paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training), a model that learns to predict which caption goes with which image by jointly training an image encoder and a text encoder to maximize cosine similarity between matched pairs and minimize it for mismatched pairs, enabling zero-shot transfer to downstream vision tasks
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: WIT (WebImageText), a dataset of 400 million (image, text) pairs collected from the internet
raw_extraction.application: Zero-shot image classification, object detection, geo-localization, action recognition, OCR, fine-grained classification
raw_extraction.research_problem: Developing scalable, task-agnostic visual models that can generalize to unseen visual concepts without task-specific training data
raw_extraction.hardware_used: 592 V100 GPUs (for RN50x64), 256 V100 GPUs (for ViT-L/14)
raw_extraction.extension: Uses linear projections to map image and text feature representations into a shared multimodal embedding space, replacing complex nonlinear transformations
